entry.title: Re: How are users validated in OB57 (Assign Users to Tole...
entry.content:
Hello @mismaeel2013  - There is a reason why SAP intentionally didn't provided the cross validation with Users, as this can be a very very wide list, and your organisation might wanted to assign to so many users, the first option is you can maintain it with blank values, which will be working for all the users. 

if cross reference was there this will be strict configuration and redundant one too as the same control you are doing at security role wise.

I hope it makes sense to you.

 

Thanks,

Ankur

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing
Re: Welcome to the Future: The 2H 2025 Home Page Redesign in SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi,

Just want to check if this the article is still valid/updated since we are now on 1H 2026. 

We are implementing the New Home Page in a couple of months (not a lot of time) and would like to know if there are any known issues that we have to be aware of.

thanks

Jenn

entry.title: Extending the Value of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation Beyond Salary Reviews
entry.content:
One common misconception about the SAP SuccessFactors Compensation module is that it can only be used for the salary review process.  While that remains its most common and leading practice use case, the module can also be used to support a broader range of adjacent HR and reward processes.

This post explores three practical ways organisations can extend the value of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation beyond the standard salary review use case.

 
Compensation Statement Generation

At the end of a compensation review cycle, forms are moved into the completion step and compensation statements are generated for employees.  In some organisations, however, the end to end salary review process is fixed, centrally determined, or applied directly in Employee Central.  In these scenarios, the Compensation module can still be used to support statement generation.

To configure a statement only template, pull the updated salaries after the fixed increase is applied in Employee Central or apply a fixed increase directly to all eligible employees in the worksheet.  Configure a one step centralised workflow, or a read only simplified workflow for business users to review outcomes, before routing worksheets to completion.  Once worksheets are completed, statements can be generated as usual based on the fixed increases.

This modified process allows organisations to generate compensation statements without running a full manager-input review cycle.  The approach takes advantage of the automated letter distribution, employee and manager statement notifications, and self service access, which are core features of the module.

 
Data Validation

Many HR and reward processes depend on accurate employee data.  When the ownership of employee data belongs to the business, Compensation worksheets and route maps can provide a structured way to support the validation activities.

A read-only form can display Employee Central data as at a defined point in time, giving your business data owners a consolidated view of the employees they are responsible for.  Instead of relying on static reporting, a workflow can be used to assign multiple reviewers and push accountability for the validity of data back to the business.  While changes or corrections to the data must take place in Employee Central, this creative use case of a compensation form template can form part of your salary review activities.

 
Simple Bonus Calculations

Variable Pay is often the preferred solution for incentive payments but if your organisation has a simple point-in-time bonus that does not require complex employee historical information, the Compensation module can be a practical alternative.  Gainshare or simple profit distribution plans are examples that often fall into this category.  In these cases, the standard lump sum field can be used to calculate a bonus payment without impacting an employee’s fixed salary component.

 

These creative solutions are just a few of the examples in which customers can use the functionality of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation for more than just a once a year review tool.  I’d love to hear in the comments other ways in which your organisation or client has used the module for creative business requirements.

entry.title: eclipse/ADT
entry.content: I have a question for everyone. When the ADT plug-in of eclipse2026.6 version performs the request activation, the ATC verification fails. The previous version will jump directly to the view page of ATC Problems, but this version will not jump but a pop-up box will prompt that the request is locked. What is the problem? Can it be the same as before?

entry.title: Re: Agent builder in Joule Studio is now generally available: Build your own Joule agents today!
entry.content: Great to see agent builder hit GA. For shops still on ECC or heavily customized S/4  where Joule Studio's reach is thinnest, we've had good results running a model-agnostic agent layer over OData/RFC-BAPI with governed write-back

entry.title: Re: ECP Custom Development
entry.content: please have a look into our Service Catalogue: https://help.sap.com/doc/service-catalog-for-employee-central-payroll-based-on-sap-human-capital-management-for-sap-erp-hcm/2505/en-US/Service_Catalog_V2.9.zip Customer are allowed: Customer can develop new objects in defined areas (NO modification of existing objects allowed! "Customer can develop new objects in defined areas - no interference with SAP standard, no impact on sizing and performance. Allowed areas are: new payroll functions, payroll operations, new custom tables, new custom infotypes, new fields for SAP-defined infotypes with customer-include, new reports, BADI implementations. Anything that could raise conflicts during upgrade or patch is not allowed. This means also using ""implicit enhancement points"" or applying notes that include modifications that are not part of a future Support package (such notes are for On Premise and must not be applied in the hosted payroll system). The development rights assigned to a customer may theoretically allow the customer to circumvent the aforementioned restrictions. Should SAP determine that modifications are made outside of the allowed areas mentioned above, SAP cannot be held responsible for adhering to the agreed SLA for EC Payroll. In such cases, the customer is responsible for all such modifications and their consequences."

entry.title: Re: Not able to generate DVM report for BW4HANA system in...
entry.content:
Please try implementing below note and then check EWA report for DVM contents.
3404595 - Provision to disable DVM Cloud Default Settings and display old EWA options instead

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Session | APAC
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to the below online webinar where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to use the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Register Now

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to the below online webinar where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: Unable to assign new GL account to custom movement ty...
entry.content: It is not possible to define any custom access sequence on automatic account assignment setup in SAP S4 hana on public cloud

entry.title: File add-ons SMERP and SMISU

Hi team

We are updating the ECC 6.0 EHP 6 system to EHP8, and theses add-ons no appears in the software download. Please could you share all files compatibility from version 610_700 path DIGITS?

 

Regards


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Download Software in Software Download Center
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Direct download software from the page

Q: What issue do you have with the direct download from the page?
A: Do you encounter 'No Data' in the download center?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: File add-ons SMERP and SMISU
entry.content:
After reviewing the SAP Note 2416326，I understand you need to download MOBILE ADD-ON FOR SAP.

Regarding your request, please refer to the screenshot to input the keyword to search it.

This should guide you directly to the relevant package.

You can also refer to KBA 1568179 - How to use and find the software downloads in SAP Software Center - SAP for Me for detail steps.

If the item still does not appear, please let me know what keyword you used and share any message you see on the page. I will review and help you get this resolved quickly. I am here to assist until you can successfully access the download.

entry.title: Re: Head Office & Branch in Customer Master
entry.content: Hello, Can you please give me which tcode you use? The attachment is not visible. Thank you

entry.title: Re: RFZALI20 schließt keine stornierten Belege aus
entry.content:
Hallo,
die Zahlungsliste (RFZALI20) muss alle Zahlungen in einem Zahlungslauf oder in einem Zahlungsträgermedienlauf melden (ohne den Parameter „Zahlungsbelegprüfung“ eines anderen Programms zu berücksichtigen). Daher finden Sie in dieser Zahlungsliste auch das stornierte Dokument.
Wenn das Feld 'Zahlungsbeleg-Verprobung' in der relevanten Variante für das Format gesetzt ist, wird der stornierte Belg nicht auf dem DTA ausgegeben, siehe bitte F1-Hilfe zum Feld:": Wenn Sie dieses Kennzeichen setzen, führt das System eine Zahlung nur dann aus, wenn der zugrundeliegende Beleg vorhanden und noch gültig ist.."
Die Liste der Zahlungen in der Datei finden Sie in FDTA ( Shift+F4 Zahlungssliste). Bitte überprüfen Sie diese Liste.

The payment list (RFZALI20) has to report all payments in a payment run or in a payment media run (not taking the parameter "Payment Document Validation" of another program into account). Therefore you will also find the reversed document in this payment list.
The list of payments in the file can be found in FDTA (see last screenshot in my attachment, button Shift+F4 Payment Summary). Please review this list.

Viele Grüße
,Mónika

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA error
entry.content:
Hi @abalasescu2 

If you search for error in the Load Status log, it will point you to the earliest error and can see a reference to Division:

This already gave indication there is something wrong with SQL query used for dimension related to Division.  Checking the Dimension tab, we have two Division related dimension that used the same query:

The SQL query itself has some issues:

Hope that helps.

entry.title: How to display Medium or Long Text instead of 15-character Short Text in SAP GR (FS Item)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'd like to ask is it possible to replace FS Item Short Text with Medium/Long Text to avoid text truncation in Group Reporting?

Since our FS Item descriptions are maintained in English, many of them:
  • exceed 15 characters
  • get truncated in reports and apps
This affects readability for end users.
Thank you in advance for your advice!

entry.title: Re: Improved Data Action scoping with the new Data Filter Parameter type
entry.content:
Nice addition! This would be even more useful if the selected date range from the story filter will be passed automatically as a parameter to the Data Action.

Currently, when a user selects or changes the date in the story filter, they must manually select the same date range again when executing the Data Action. Automatically passing the story filter's date selection to the Data Action parameters would streamline the process, reduce manual effort, and help prevent inconsistencies between the story filter and the Data Action.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports Developer Version 14-04-0-5335
entry.content:
How are you connecting to the XLXS files?

You can enable CR's database logging component to see what is happening:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/1603398

Look for the string length, CR is querying the Client to get the length, it's likely returning 65k or more, you'll have to go to makers to fix it.

I don't see xlxs as a data source in CR Database wizard:

So likely cause is the Excel client you are using is returning the length, likely 65K.

A quick Google search for this, see if MS's client works for you:

1. Install the Drivers [1]
  • Most Windows systems require the installation of the Microsoft Access Database Engine Redistributable. This package installs the necessary 32-bit or 64-bit OLEDB and ODBC drivers for modern Office extensions (.xlsx). [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
 
2. Configure the ODBC Data Source (DSN) [1]
  1. Open the Windows Start menu and search for ODBC Data Sources.
  2. Choose 64-bit or 32-bit to strictly match the architecture of the application reading the data.
  3. Under the User DSN or System DSN tab, click Add....
  4. Select Microsoft Excel Driver (*.xls, *.xlsx, *.xlsm, *.xlsb) and click Finish.
  5. Provide a memorable Data Source Name (DSN).
  6. Click Select Workbook..., navigate to your target .xlsx file, and confirm. [1, 2, 3, 4]

Don

entry.title: Re: Understanding field(suppress) in SAP RAP and How It Differs from @UI.hidden and @Consumption.hid
entry.content: Your explanation of how field (suppress) works at the RAP behavior layer is extremely clear and practical. I especially liked the comparison with @ui.hidden and readonly, and the runtime observations you shared about EML structures and draft handling. This really helps clarify when suppression is the safer option for protecting audit fields in RAP-based applications.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic height control of Tabstrip in classic Dynpro — is it possible?
entry.content: Dynpro can’t resize tabstrips dynamically. The usual workaround is to keep the container fixed and just load different subscreens based on the tab. If you truly need dynamic resizing, you’d have to move to control framework or a modern UI.

entry.title: Re: SAC Writeback to Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @RobertBoys ,

Please kindly review the steps mentioned in the help document:

Create an SAP Datasphere Cloud Data Integration Connection to Your SAP Analytics Cloud Tenant

entry.title: Re: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Hi @dawid90,

I get product master data using RETAIL_ST_PL_PRODUCT_ENH badi and assign value to custom field (this field is added via Custom Fields app). This field is also exposed to RETAILSTORE_PRODUCT_LKP_SRV services.

If I want to bind this field value in UI, how to bind this? I added like this in xml file but it doesn't work. Is there any reference syntax for this?

Logic in RETAIL_ST_PL_PRODUCT_ENH badi

METHOD if_retail_st_pl_product_enh~get_product_supp_data.

DATA lv_product_gp TYPE string.

IF ct_product-matnr IS NOT INITIAL.

SELECT SINGLE ProductGroup

FROM I_Product with privileged access

WHERE Product = @ct_product-matnr

INTO @lv_product_gp.

ct_product-yy1_productdescriptio2_lkp = lv_product_gp.

ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

XML code in BAS

 

Thank You.

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Re: Wrapping a Custom REST API as a Joule Skill using SAP Build & Joule Studio
entry.content: @Sharathmg thankk you... Is this joule studio 1.0 available in SAP BTP for us to activate or should we wait for 2.0 ... Usually SAP sunsets often when we have fully integrated to our landscape or thinking of what to purchase 1.0 vs 2.0?

entry.title: 【SAP For Me/S User】SAPからの通知メール内「EVBxxxxxxxx」の内容詳細を表示する権限の不足
entry.content:
【SAP For Me/S User】SAPからの通知メール内「EVBxxxxxxxx」の内容詳細を表示する権限の不足

対象環境: CLOUD / cba3f6a9-29e5-440d-8c77-c910fb68b626 / Production

受信メール件名:　SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime — Deprecation and Upcoming Removal of TLS 1.2 Ciphers

内容詳細:

特定のSユーザで、SAPから受信した上記通知メール内リンク「EVBxxxxxxxx」へアクセスし、
SAP For Me ->Systems & Provisioning ->EVBxxxxxxxx　の詳細を確認しようとしたところ
権限不足により「制限付きカードコンテンツ」と表示され、閲覧が出来ない状態となっております。

上記画面内「権限依頼」を押下/又は対象カードの権限詳細情報を確認することで
「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」権限が必要とありますが、どちらかのみで問題ないでしょうか。
またはどちらの権限も付与が必要でしょうか。
他に付与が必要な権限がありましたらご教示ください。
参照ページ: About the User, Authorization and Administrator Concept 
　　　　　DIGITS - 特定のカードを使用するために必要な S ユーザ ID 権限の決定方法- SAP for Me - SAP for Me 

また、弊社では「システムデータの表示/編集」権限付与の際は、対象のインストレーションによって管理・許可しているため
対象メールのカードを表示するにあたりインストレーションを適切に付与する必要があります。
本件はSAP BTP関連の通知のため、以下インストレーションが該当すると考えておりますが、正否についてご教示ください。
インストレーション:　DIGITS(SAP CLOUD PLATFORM)

付与対象のSユーザ情報、閲覧不可となっているSAP通知メール等の詳細情報につきましては、ドキュメントにまとめ、後ほどアップロードいたします。

※本ケースの対象コンポーネントが誤っている場合は、お手数ですが適切なコンポーネントへのご転送をお願いいたします。

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 【SAP For Me/S User】SAPからの通知メール内「EVBxxxxxxxx」の内容詳細を表示す...
entry.content:
ご質問について、以下の回答をご確認するようにお願い申し上げます。

1.「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」権限が必要とありますが、どちらかのみで問題ないでしょうか。またはどちらの権限も付与が必要でしょうか。他に付与が必要な権限がありましたらご教示ください。

--->はい、どちらかのみで問題ないです。該当KBA 2587408 - S-user ID authorizations for Cloud Availability and Manage Cloud Service Availability Notifications (Get Notified) - SAP for Meをご確認ください。

該当内容を抜粋致します、ご参考いただければと思います。

In order to see your cloud products and systems for a particular installation, your S-user ID must have one of the following authorizations. They can be set at the cloud installation number level, customer number level, or CCC level.

Display System Data
Edit System Data

つまり、「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」権限をどちらのみで付与すれば問題ないです。

2.本件はSAP BTP関連の通知のため、以下インストレーションが該当すると考えておりますが、正否についてご教示ください。インストレーション:　*

--->はい、インストレーション:　*該当です。

３．作業実行ユーザ：*

--->弊社バックエンドで調べておりますが、そのS-user様は自分がインストレーション *にて該当権限を持っていないです。

ユーザー管理のルール上、管理者は自身に割り当てられた権限のみを管理可能となっております。そのS-user様は当該インストール番号*に対する権限を保有していないため、「システムデータの編集」又は「システムデータの編集」の権限を操作することができません。当該ユーザーの権限は他のインストール番号に紐付けられています。

*様に権限を付与する必要がある場合は、の当該インストール番号のクラウド管理者（Cloud Admin）までご連絡ください。

entry.title: Re: SAP UX Update: The New Joule Work Engagement Layer Experiences
entry.content:
@Jocelyn_Dart @In back olden days there was no concept of EAC.. we used to try and experiment with BTP Trial account of 90days to understand the product or services better and choose or buy from SAP.. but going with EAC.. there are lot of overheads and system admin to be involved.. discussions needs to take place...

I don't think going with EAC... Very few will opt for it....

entry.title: Re: Joule A2A: Async Push Notifications for Long-Running Agents
entry.content:
@felixbartler my point is we hear alot about Joule... Many names around it

Joule Studio 1.0

Joule studio 2.0

Joule for consultants 

Joule Base version

Joule Premium version

Joule work

Joule Space

CX AI Toolkit (Joule Icon in CX Applications)

Joule Skills

All of this does something different and as developer in our company what is the right choice and what gives us benefit and all this are not available in BTP Trial account for 90days to understand better.. so we can know what's good and not good for choice... Since soo many names it's confuses

entry.title: Reversing the legacy Data Transfer - due to changes of posting date
entry.content:
We have created a Legacy Data Transfer Project in FAA_CMP with a posting date of 01.04.2026 and have already completed the asset transfer posting.

The business has now requested that the legacy transfer date be changed to 31.03.2026 instead.

Could you please confirm whether the following approach is feasible?

 

  1.  Reverse the legacy asset transfer posting that was made with the posting date 01.04.2026.
  2.   Complete/close the existing Legacy Data Transfer Project created with the date 01.04.2026.
  3.   Create a new Legacy Data Transfer Project in FAA_CMP with the posting date 31.03.2026.
  4.   Re-upload and post the legacy asset data using the new project.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Identity Services Bug in SAP BTP Trial Environment: Current Status
entry.content:
@Maria_Radde @abdul_rahman5 @Vikram_1423 @sasank_mandava-01 @deepu12345 

It's more than month it's been going on.... Does SAP takes this long even in production is what we dont  get... As understood BTP Trial account is provided for new developers or from non SAP background who wants to experience and gain knowledge but we face this kind of issues we dont get intrested to ,move forward with SAP technologies... Just broken piece to fix

Because of this issue you don't even extend the BTP trial validity dates... Who is the sufferer? We lost 30days from the given 90days

entry.title: Application could not be started due to technical issues. "h.loaded is not a function"
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm facing an issue with a RAP Fiori Elements application running in SAP Build Work Zone.

Scenario

  1. Open my My Inbox (Integrate with SAP Build Process Automation).
  2. Go back to the Work Zone home (or use the browser Back button).
  3. Open the RAP application.

The application fails to start and shows the following error:

Has anyone experienced a similar issue with SAP Build Work Zone or Launchpad navigation?

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: F-90: CREDIT NOTE ISSUE
entry.content:
Dear Team,

WE have an asset 14100293 under company code 3000. It has two esxternal asset acquisition on dated 31.07.2025 & 04.02.2026.By mistekenly we

have transerfer the value of acqusition of dated 04.02.2026 . Now we are craeting an credit note of acqusition value 31.07.2025 and we are 
encountring an issue below mentioned 

Posting transaction is not possible (value date not allowed)

Message no. AA478

Kindly provide a solution on this and i have attached a schreenshot for your refernce.

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Translate FIORI tile's Title/Group Name
entry.content: After changing the language from English to French in the SAP Fiori Launchpad, the tile titles and group names are appearing blank.

entry.title: Re: In S/4HANA EHS Task Framework, how to find whether an...
entry.content:
Hello,

there is no indicator available to check whether a Task Definition in the EHS Task Management has any attachments. However, you can use the class CL_ODATA_CV_ATTACHMENT_API (method: RETRIEVE_ATTACHMENTS_ID) to get a list of all attachments associated to a Task Definition. There are several example uses of the class in standard EHS classes, such as CL_EHFND_TASK_UTIL or CL_EHFND_TASK_DEF_D_DEL_ATT.

The parameters for the class/method above are quite straight forward. You have to pass the UUID of the task (KEY of EHFNDD_TDEF_ROOT, in some other places the field is called EHSTaskDefinitionUUID) and the Object Type (which is used to identify the "hosting object" of the attachment - this is a constant value).

With kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with SAP Joule for Consultants - Setup Guide
entry.content: Here is a video how to activate J4C which shared in the customer bootcamp - https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_s2flkbzr

entry.title: Re: Fiori App 'Clear Outgoing Payments' (F1367) - Default Document Type 'KZ'
entry.content: Same problem here, did anyone find a solution for this?

entry.title: Decentralized Extended Warehouse Management and Quality Management in Two-Tier ERP
entry.content:
The SAP Community blog by @Sahil_Grover introduces a hybrid integration between decentralized SAP EWM and SAP Cloud ERP.

  • SAP EWM Integration - Inbound Processing from Supplier & Outbound Processing to Customer

The SAP Extended Warehouse Management Integration with SAP Cloud ERP - Part 1

  • Setup for The SAP Extended Warehouse Management Integration with SAP Cloud ERP

The SAP Extended Warehouse Management Integration with SAP Cloud ERP - Part 2

In this blog, we introduce a new scenario for SAP Decentralized EWM integration with Quality Management, where Quality Inspection is executed directly in EWM using the Quality Inspection Engine (QIE).

This approach is especially relevant for businesses that want to perform inspection decisions closer to warehouse execution, reduce dependency on ERP-based inspection activities, and enable faster Inbound processing.

Business Scenario Overview

In this scenario:

  • A Purchase order and Inbound delivery are created in SAP Cloud ERP
  • The Inbound delivery is distributed to the Decentralized EWM
  • Goods receipt is posted in EWM
  • Quality inspection is triggered and executed directly in EWM
  • Inspection results and follow-up actions are performed within EWM
  • Stock status is updated and synchronized with SAP Cloud ERP

The key differentiator here is that inspection happens exactly where the goods are physically handled — in the warehouse.

Key Configuration Focus Areas

To enable this scenario successfully, some important setup areas need attention.

  1. Quality Inspection Engine (QIE) in EWM

The Inspection object type, Inspection rules, and decision logic need to be configured correctly so that Inspection documents are created and processed as expected.

       2. Product and Warehouse Master Data

The warehouse product master and material setup must support inspection-relevant processing. Stock determination and warehouse execution behavior should align with the quality process.

       3. Delivery Integration Between SAP Cloud ERP and EWM

Inbound delivery distribution must work correctly so that the warehouse receives the required execution documents in time.

       4. Follow-Up Action Determination

Accepted, Rejected, and Exception outcomes should be mapped carefully to the right stock types and warehouse actions.

       5. Warehouse Process Integration

Inspection may be required at goods receipt, before putaway, at handling unit level, or in work center-related steps depending on the business design.

A well-designed configuration ensures that warehouse execution remains smooth and the quality process behaves consistently.

Process Flow

  1. Purchase Order and Inbound Delivery in SAP Cloud ERP

The process begins in ERP with procurement. Once the inbound delivery is created, it is distributed to the decentralized EWM system for warehouse execution.

       2. Goods Receipt in in Decentralized EWM

When the goods arrive physically, the warehouse posts goods receipt in EWM. At this point, inspection-relevant checks are triggered.

        3. Inspection Document Creation via QIE

Based on configured rules, EWM creates an inspection document using the Quality Inspection Engine. This document becomes the operational object for the warehouse-level inspection.

        4. Inspection Execution in EWM

The inspection is performed directly in EWM by the warehouse or quality user. This may include visual checks, packaging verification, quantity checks, damage checks, or handling-unit-level validation.

The warehouse user records the inspection result in EWM and completes the inspection process without switching to ERP for operational execution.

This is particularly useful in decentralized warehouse scenarios where speed and execution simplicity are critical.

       5. Usage Decision / Inspection Outcome in EWM

After inspection execution, the user records the final inspection outcome in EWM.

Typical outcomes include: Accepted, Rejected, Partially accepted, Rework required, Return to vendor, and Scrap

        6. Follow-Up Action in EWM

After the inspection result is recorded, EWM automatically determines the next action. Stock can be released to Unrestricted use, Blocked, routed for rework, returned to Supplier, or scrapped based on the configured decision logic

Conclusion

The 7L1-based Two-Tier Decentralized EWM scenario with EWM-based Quality Inspection is a strong option for businesses that want inspection to happen closer to warehouse execution.

By using QIE in EWM, organizations can inspect inbound goods directly in the warehouse, record decisions immediately, and trigger the right follow-up action without delaying operational flow.

In a Two-Tier SAP architecture, this approach provides a practical balance between central ERP control and local warehouse execution efficiency.

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
entry.content:
The CDS compiler is a very capable mechanism and can advise and warn us if we present questionable design choices in our models. It can even refuse to continue in extreme circumstances.

Let's say we have a Book entity defined thus:

entity Book {
  key  id : String;
     isbn : String;
  content : String;
};

Then, in a service definition, we create a projection on that entity like this:

entity IsbnBook as projection on Book {
  *,
  isbn as id
};

The questionable design choice here is that with the isbn as id, we're effectively overwriting the id element that we're including by means of the * wildcard.

The compiler will issue a message with a severity level of Info for this. What is the message identifier (it is a three word phrase where the words are joined by dashes (-)?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: CAMT53 into API structure for communication arrangement : SAP_COM_0316 ( NO MBC)
entry.content:
We are in the process of implementing the incoming CAMT53 statement from Bank. In this regard, we understand that there is provision in MBC. But, without MBC, we are using SAP_COM_0316 communication arrangement. However, what would be best way to map the incoming CAMT53 with API Structure. I tried to map the XML with API structure, but not able to find the transaction details int he API structure.

I understand that one check the missing tag in XML by uploading the same in the Test Incoming Format Mapping :

entry.title: Re: Advanced Scripting in SAC: How to use Page Navigation & Popup Panels
entry.content:
Dear Priya_312,
thank you so much for this insightful post on scripting in SAC! I really appreciate the do’s and don’ts - sometimes less truly is more.
I’ve actually seen in one company that programmers were building the SAC stories, and they laughed at just 4 lines of code…in their case, it was more like 400! The consequences were significant: unstable stories, issues during QRCs, and a complete lack of transparency around what was happening in SAC. Conclusion: No real benefit - not for developers, not for users!
Thanks again for sharing these important insights!

entry.title: Updating Sales Order Header and Sales Order Item using Business Object Framework(Cleancore Approach)
entry.content:
In RAP, business processes are encapsulated within Business Objects (BOs), which define the data model and business logic. Developers interact with these Business Objects using the Entity Manipulation Language (EML), a modern ABAP syntax that supports operations such as CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, and EXECUTE. By leveraging RAP and EML, applications become more maintainable, extensible, and upgrade-safe while adhering to SAP's Clean Core strategy and cloud-ready development standards.

Example : - 

 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp

ENTITY salesorder

UPDATE FIELDS ( zz1_testdata_sdh )

WITH VALUE #( (

%key-salesorder = keys[ 1 ]-salesorder

zz1_testdata_sdh = 'TestingData'

) )

ENTITY salesorderitem

UPDATE FIELDS ( zz1_status1_sdi )

WITH VALUE #(

(

%key-salesorder = keys[ 1 ]-salesorder

%key-salesorderitem = keys[ 1 ]-itemno

zz1_status1_sdi = keys[ 1 ]-%param-status

)

).

 

Note: The approach described here is not limited to custom fields. It applies to any field exposed by the SalesOrder or SalesOrderItem Business Object, regardless of whether the field is standard or custom.

If you introduce a custom field by directly appending standard database tables (for example, VBAK or VBAP), you must manually extend the entire data exposure layer. This typically involves extending CDS views, updating OData services, enhancing APIs, and ensuring the field is propagated across all relevant artifacts, which increases development effort and maintenance complexity.

A more Clean Core–compliant approach is to create custom fields using the Custom Fields and Logic (CFL) framework. When configured and enabled correctly, CFL automatically extends the relevant CDS views, OData services, and Business Objects, significantly reducing manual development. To ensure end-to-end availability of the custom field, enable the required UIs, SAP GUI, CDS Views, and APIs during field creation. For implementing custom business logic, use Cloud BAdIs instead of classical enhancement techniques.

For a detailed walkthrough on enabling CFL artifacts and implementing Cloud BAdIs, please refer to my previous blog.

- https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-field-and-logic-from-scratch/ba-p/14426888

entry.title: Re: Updating Sales Order Header and Sales Order Item using Business Object Framework(Cleancore Appro
entry.content:
You can directly update item as well- 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp

ENTITY salesorderitem

UPDATE FIELDS ( zz1_status1_sdi )

WITH VALUE #(

(

%key-salesorder = keys[ 1 ]-salesorder

%key-salesorderitem = keys[ 1 ]-itemno

zz1_status1_sdi = keys[ 1 ]-%param-status

)

)

FAILED DATA(lt_failed)

REPORTED DATA(lt_reported).

entry.title: Re: Updating Sales Order Header and Sales Order Item using Business Object Framework(Cleancore Appro
entry.content:
This example is for update but it's valid for Complete CRUD Operation

 

-->>> You can directly update item as well 

MODIFY ENTITIES OF I_SalesOrderTP

ENTITY SalesOrderItem

UPDATE FIELDS ( ZZ1_STATUS1_SDI )

WITH VALUE #( (
%key-SalesOrder = keys[ 1 ]-SalesOrder
%key-SalesOrderItem = keys[ 1 ]-ItemNo
ZZ1_STATUS1_SDI = keys[ 1 ]-%param-Status
) )

FAILED DATA(lt_failed)

REPORTED DATA(lt_reported).

entry.title: HTTP 403 Forbidden when SAP CPI calls On-Premise HTTPS API via Cloud Connector.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to connect SAP Integration Suite (CPI) to a third-party REST API that is hosted on an on-premise server.

The third party has exposed the API over HTTPS with port 443, and the API works successfully when called directly from Postman using the HTTPS URL. This API does not require any kind of headers. 

To access the on-premise system from CPI, I am using SAP Cloud Connector. In my integration flow, I have configured the receiver using the Cloud Connector virtual host. 
I am using HTTP receiver adapter with HTTP protocol because Proxy type On-Premise does not allow HTTPS.
We are calling API with HTTP  protocol and port 80.
Has anyone faced a similar issue where an HTTPS API works from Postman but returns 403 only when called through SAP Cloud Connector with HTTP protocol?


Cloud connector is Reachable.

entry.title: Re: SAC: Measure-Based Dimension vs Calculated Dimension
entry.content:
Hi egil63,

Add the Measure Input Control, select the required range of value and hide the Widget.

entry.title: Individual MRP - Check unrestricted stock in individual MRP
entry.content:
Hi,

We are in EPC Business (Engineering, Procure, Commission). we are using Make to Order scenario.

I want to track everything from Sales Order like PR, PO, Delivery, Production and that's why we use Individual MRP.

Some of raw material commonly use in most of order which we do bulk purchase.

I want that while i run individual MRP, MRP will check unrestricted stock of that RAW Material.

#MRP #IndividualMRP #PP #production

entry.title: Re: Custom Field and Logic from Scratch
entry.content:
If you want to update it from RAP using Business Object Framework - I_SALESORDERTP is the most important thing to enable.  Then you don't need bapi to update it. 

#cleancore

entry.title: Re: When adding MAKZ and MAKV Tables/Fields in MASSOBJ fo...
entry.content: As per SAP note 2867655 there is a blank entry in table MASSFLDLST which you can delete via SE16N > View Maintenance

entry.title: Background Job for ME05
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have a requirement to generate the Source List automatically. For this  we have created a variant for ME05 and scheduled it as a background job.

The initial selection screen is executed successfully in the background without any issues. However, after the initial screen  ME05 displays a second screen containing the list of records. On this screen, we need to select all line items and click the Save button to generate the Source List.

Is there any standard way to achieve this in a background job? If so, please share the approach. Any suggestions would be appreciated.

 

Advance Thanks.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
Badi for the Stock Information message at PR level
entry.content:
Dear Team,

The client has a requirement to display the available stock of a material as an informational message at two stages:

  1. During Purchase Requisition (PR) creation
  2. At the My Inbox (approver) level

Is there any body where we can make this customization by using BADI.

Thank you

entry.title: Projecting WBS Element onto Customer Line Items
entry.content:
We are currently unable to view the WBS Element on Customer Line Items within standard operational apps, and we need guidance on the standard configuration to enable this.

During a customer invoice posting (e.g., Journal Entry 9400000112), the WBS Element is successfully populated on the P&L line item (Item 000002 shows WBS 'UAE/ACC/23/WO' in I_ACCOUNTINGDOCUMENTJOURNAL). However, the Customer/AR reconciliation line (Item 000001) leaves the WBS Element field blank. Consequently, the WBS Element does not appear when viewing the invoice in the "Manage Customer Line Items" app

entry.title: Re: Badi for the Stock Information message at PR level
entry.content:
Yes, this requirement can be achieved, but there is no standard BAdI specifically meant to display available stock as an informational message in both scenarios. The implementation approach depends on whether the client is using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Private Cloud, or On-Premise.
1. During Purchase Requisition (PR) Creation
If the PR is created using the Manage Purchase Requisitions Fiori app or ME51N equivalent:
You can implement one of the following enhancement spots:
• BAdI: MMPUR_S4_PR_CHECK (S/4HANA)
o Used to validate Purchase Requisition data before saving.
o You can read the available stock using CDS views or APIs and display an Information (I) message.
o This is the recommended enhancement point for PR validations.
Alternatively:
• ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST (for On-Premise/GUI transactions)
o Method: PROCESS_ITEM
o Read material, plant, storage location.
o Fetch stock from MATDOC, I_MaterialStock, or MMIM stock APIs.
o Raise an informational message.
________________________________________
2. At My Inbox (Approver) Level
This is more challenging because My Inbox is a generic workflow application.
There is no standard MM BAdI that allows you to inject custom messages directly into the My Inbox approval screen.
Possible approaches are:
• Extend the CDS/OData service used by the approval app to expose current stock.
• Extend the UI using a Fiori extension.
• Implement a workflow enhancement or determination to display stock information.
• Use a side panel or custom section to show available stock.
Displaying a popup/information message in My Inbox via BAdI is generally not supported in standard SAP.
________________________________________
Stock Retrieval Logic
Regardless of the enhancement point, available stock can be obtained from:
• CDS View I_MaterialStock
• CDS View I_StockQuantityCurrentValue
• Table MATDOC (S/4HANA)
• Tables MARD/MCHB (depending on the stock type)
• Standard MM APIs
________________________________________
Recommendation
Requirement Suggested Enhancement
PR Creation BAdI MMPUR_S4_PR_CHECK (preferred) or ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST (On-Premise)
My Inbox Approval Fiori extension or Workflow enhancement (no standard BAdI to display informational messages)
Clarification Required
To suggest the exact BAdI, could you please confirm:
1. Is your system SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Private Cloud, or On-Premise?
2. Is the Purchase Requisition created using the Manage Purchase Requisitions Fiori app or ME51N?
3. Is the approval performed through the standard My Inbox Fiori app or a custom approval application?
Once these details are confirmed, I can provide the exact enhancement spot and a sample ABAP implementation.

However I noticed that strangely in bseg even while being in a user exit at header level the EBELN field is filled with the PO number. So we were able to fill the reference with the PO number. entry.title: Re: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content: What to do if SIREN is not showing as active in  Chorus Platform? Registration in Peppol network is successful

entry.title: Re: Connect to ldap server in CAP (nodeJS) project on BTP
entry.content:
We had a similar requirement, and we couldn't achieve it using LDAP alone. We eventually resolved the issue by switching to Azure AD (Microsoft Entra ID) and using the Microsoft Graph API.

The high-level steps are:

  1. Create an Azure account (if you don't already have one) and register an application in the Azure portal. Make sure this aligns with your organization's security and IT policies.
  2. Obtain the Application (Client) ID and Tenant ID for your application.
  3. Configure the required Microsoft Graph API permissions. By default, we only had basic user permissions, but we had to request Admin Consent to grant broader permissions (for example, to read all users).
  4. Generate an access token using Azure AD.
  5. Use the Microsoft Graph SDK to query the required user information.

The following npm packages were used in our implementation:

@azure/identity
@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client

Below is a sample implementation that demonstrates the approach:

```typescript
import { ClientSecretCredential } from "@azure/identity";
import { Client } from "@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client";

async function createGraphClient() {
  const credential = new ClientSecretCredential(
    "<TENANT_ID>",
    "<CLIENT_ID>",
    "<CLIENT_SECRET>"
  );

  return Client.initWithMiddleware({
    authProvider: {
      getAccessToken: async () => {
        const token = await credential.getToken(
          "https://graph.microsoft.com/.default"
        );
        return token?.token ?? "";
      },
    },
  });
}

async function getUsersByCostCenter(costCenter: string) {
  const client = await createGraphClient();

  const response = await client
    .api("/users")
    .select("id,displayName,mail")
    .filter(
      `onPremisesExtensionAttributes/extensionAttribute2 eq '${costCenter}'`
    )
    .get();

  return response.value;
}

// Example
const users = await getUsersByCostCenter("10001");
console.log(users);
```

This approach worked reliably for our use case after LDAP proved insufficient.

entry.title: Re: Unable to access BI reports via LPD_CUST configuratio...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out.

We would like to inform you that this application type is no longer supported. Please refer to the resources below for further guidance on the recommended approach:

  • SAP Note 2614740 — Fiori Launchpad Designer: Usage of Application Type "SAP Fiori App using LPD_CUST" in Target Mapping Configuration is Deprecated
  • About Launchpad Content | Tiles

We recommend reviewing the above materials and transitioning to a supported configuration at your earliest convenience.

Please do not hesitate to reach out if you have any further questions — we are happy to assist.

Best regards，
Joe

New installations were created

Following installations were created:
SMARTRECRUITERS FOR SAP SFSF (009110XXXX)

 

when we click on the link "show in application" we are redirected to me.sap.com where there is no system number, no platform, no contact

entry.title: Public Cloud - How to post a prior-year direct supplier invoice for an asset when MM period is close
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, our client forgot to post a fixed asset purchased in June 2025. The asset master is already created, and now they want to record the acquisition with the original posting date (June 2025).

When we use Create Supplier Invoice or Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (direct invoice without PO), we receive Message M8022 stating that the MM posting period is not allowed, even though the FI posting period is open.

The Post Asset Acquisition app allows the posting, but it does not create the supplier liability.

Is there any standard way in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to post a prior-year direct supplier invoice for an asset (Dr Asset / Cr Supplier), or is Post Asset Acquisition the only supported option in this scenario?

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
entry.content: c8a54f1683a934a2df94c09b8fb3ce44cfafffbcece08eced62514ea399eeb5c

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Multi-approver workflo...
entry.content:
Hello Henrike,

Thank you very much for your response and for taking the time to provide such detailed guidance — it is really appreciated.

I just wanted to clarify one point regarding item 3 (workflow configuration), as I believe there might be a small inaccuracy.

As far as I understand, both standard workflows:

  • Workflow for Blocked Invoices, and
  • Workflow for Parked Supplier Invoices as Complete

do support cost center-based logic to some extent. Specifically:

  • As start condition, you can use “Account Assignment Category ‘Cost Center’ is used” = Yes
  • And for recipients, you can use “Persons Responsible for All Used Cost Centers”

So, there is indeed a standard way to route approval based on the cost center.

However, the limitation I am facing is that: 👉 the cost center master data only allows a single “Responsible Person”,
which prevents defining multiple approvers per cost center, which is the actual requirement.

So the gap is not so much in triggering the workflow based on the cost center, but rather in supporting multiple approvers for the same cost center within the standard functionality.

That said, thanks again for pointing me to the flexible workflow and for the overall explanation — it was very helpful.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: CAMT53 into API structure for communication arrangeme...
entry.content:
Bank
│
CAMT.053 XML
│
Middleware (CPI/PI/PO/Other)
│
SOAP API: BANKSTATEMENTPOST_IN
│
Communication Arrangement: SAP_COM_0316
│
Manage Bank Messages
│
Manage Bank Statements

entry.title: Re: CAMT53 into API structure for communication arrangeme...
entry.content:
Without MBC

You do not need SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity if:

You receive the CAMT.053 file directly from the bank.
Your middleware converts or forwards the XML to the SOAP service.
You configure Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0316.

entry.title: Re: issue to deploy 3 copies of the standard iflows packa...
entry.content:
We faced simialr issues several  times with SAP standard content. In our scenarios most often we use predefined customer exit points in the iflows to handle the value mappings via those customer exit iflows.

I just checked the iflow https://api.sap.com/integrationflow/com.sap.PA_SE_IN.ec2erp.SFSFtoSAPGenericObjectReplication.V1 

and could see that there is no customer exit point available. But maybe you can raise this topic via SAP Influence or SAP Incident and they will provide a new version of the iflow soon. Otherwise you need to create a custom copy (not updatable) of the iflow and do your changes there to point to a separate mapping for each of the 3 system specific iflows

Re: Hello, could someone help me with choosing the correc...
entry.content: Please use IP43.

entry.title: Posting negative stock enabled materials with Perform Maintenance Job app
entry.content:
Hi Community,

We have enabled negative stock in Material Master and also in transaction OMJ1. When trying to post the material with Perform Maintenance Job application (F5104A) the app doesn't allow the posting because there is not quantity in stock. But the posting can be performed with the old transaction code IW42 with completed goods movement. 

So with IW42 it works but not with Perform Maintenance Job app.

entry.title: Attachments in Quality Notification emails are not included when I receive the email.
entry.content:
I am experiencing an issue where attachments in Quality Notification emails are not attached when I receive the email. I have reviewed common issues and configuration settings but the problem persists. 

One of the functionalities of Quality Notification does not work because the attachment is not sent along with the email. In QM02, you can attach any document using this:

 

 

 

Go to “Attachment List”

 

 

Afterwards, you will see all attachments:

 

 

 

To send:

in QM02, under the Action Box, there is "Send Mail with Attachment".

 

 

Click “Select all Attachments” and click “Yes”.

 

 

 

Enter LOCL as Output Device and Press Continue.

 

 

Put some details:

 

 

And then click “Send” icon.

At this point, you will be prompted with pop-up window and displaying the attachment in the Quality Notification.

 

IMPORTANT: Please take note that we have ALL settings as mentioned in 2564368 - QM02- Unable to send mail with attachment via activity box | How to configure 'Sending Notifications with Attachments'? - SAP for Me.

Still the issue is not resolved.

 

 

Click “Check”.

 

Then choose the partner and click check.

 

 

And then click “Check”.

However, upon checking the email, there the selected attachment from Quality Notification is not attached in to the email.

entry.title: Universal Journal inconsistency after ledger-specific posting
entry.content:
Why does ACDOCA show different profitability segment values across ledgers for the same accounting document?

Hi Experts,

We are using Universal Parallel Accounting with multiple extension ledgers.

For the same accounting document in ACDOCA, profitability segment (PAOBJNR) values differ across leading and non-leading ledgers even though derivation logic is identical.

Is this expected behavior or a system limitation in ledger-specific derivation of CO-PA characteristics?

entry.title: Re: How are users validated in OB57 (Assign Users to Tole...
entry.content: I got your point with blank tolerance grOup, but my question is still here, lets say i need to assign a non-blank tolerance and I write a name of a user in OB57, so how do i know as a consultant that the tolerance is assigned to the correct intended user. how can i verify it? since we are not maintaining it in any sort of user master data.. or we are not having the same list. A typo mistake can also occur..so how do I verify the details

entry.title: MDG UI: Recently used values displayed in dropdown but cannot be selected
entry.content: In SAP MDG UI (accessed through NWBC), previously used values are displayed in the dropdown/history list for input fields. However, users are unable to select these values.

entry.title: Re: Background Job for ME05
entry.content:
Hello Mahil,

this should not be a problem in principle, if the transaction is executed in the background, it saves the changes. I did a test with a material and it works fine, but I chose "all records for Material / Plant" and the option "delete".

As I understand it, you have already tested this and it doesn't work??

Regards Jan

entry.title: Two Decades of Proven Excellence: Syntax Achieves SAP Golden Certificate While Driving Innovation
entry.content:
How do you keep mission-critical SAP environments running flawlessly while continuously pushing innovation forward? In this interview, Syntax shares how its deep SAP heritage shapes a modern, global approach to operations – and marks a major milestone along the way. By achieving SAP certification for the 10th consecutive time, Syntax has earned the prestigious Golden Certificate, underlining its long-standing commitment to quality and operational excellence. Combined with newly achieved global certifications in SAP Security Operations and SAP Business AI Operations, this recognition highlights Syntax's ability to deliver secure, future-ready services at scale. From cloud transformation to the growing role of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and AI, discover how Syntax helps customers turn SAP operations into a true driver of business value.

Could you start by telling us about your company and the services you offer?

Syntax is a global technology solutions and services provider with deep expertise in running and transforming mission-critical enterprise environments. We help customers manage complexity across cloud, applications, infrastructure, security, and end-user services, with SAP being one of our core strategic focus areas.

In the SAP space, we support customers across the full lifecycle: from advisory, migration, and implementation through to technical managed services, application management, optimization, and innovation. That includes operating highly business-critical SAP landscapes, supporting transformation to SAP S/4HANA and cloud-based environments, and helping customers unlock additional value through SAP BTP, automation, security, and AI.

What differentiates Syntax is our Boutique @ Scale approach. The ability to combine strong operational discipline with a forward-looking innovation mindset, tailoring every engagement to our customers' unique priorities. Customers come to us not only to keep their SAP environments stable and secure, but to evolve them as business needs change, backed by the agility and personal care of a boutique, delivered with the reach and resilience of a global enterprise.

 

 

Your SAP roots run deep, brought to Syntax through Freudenberg IT, whose SAP pedigree reaches back almost to SAP's founding. How has that heritage shaped who Syntax is today as an SAP operations partner – and is it still something you draw on?

Our heritage through Freudenberg IT has had a lasting impact on who we are as an SAP operations partner. It shaped a culture centered on reliability, accountability, process discipline, and long-term customer commitment, all of which remain essential when you are operating business-critical SAP environments.

That background gave us a very practical understanding of what enterprise customers need from an operations partner: not just technical expertise, but consistency, governance, responsiveness, and the ability to support transformation without compromising stability.

We absolutely still draw on that heritage today. While Syntax has evolved into a global organization with a broad portfolio and a modern cloud- and innovation-focused vision, the core DNA remains the same: trusted operations, deep SAP knowledge, and a commitment to helping customers run mission-critical systems with confidence.

 

Looking back at those early years of the partnership, what inspired your decision to seek your first service certification in 2002 – initially in Germany – and what then drove the decision to expand that certification globally in 2006?

From the beginning, we saw certification not simply as a badge, but as an important way to benchmark ourselves against SAP's own operational standards and to validate the quality of the services we were delivering to customers. Starting in Germany made sense because that was where our SAP operations heritage was strongest and where we had built deep experience in supporting complex customer landscapes. As customer expectations grew and our delivery model became increasingly international, expanding that certification globally was the next natural step. It reflected two priorities: first, giving customers the confidence that they would receive the same high-quality service wherever they operated; and second, creating a globally consistent operational framework across our teams. In that sense, certification supported both trust and scale.

Handover of the golden certificate, June 2026

 

How would you describe Syntax's SAP operations proposition to a customer who doesn't want to choose between operational reliability and continuous innovation?

Our message would be very simple: you should not have to choose between the two. At Syntax, we believe the strongest SAP operations model is one that protects the business today while enabling transformation for tomorrow. That means delivering stable, secure, and highly governed day-to-day operations while also helping customers adopt new capabilities in areas such as cloud, automation, SAP BTP, and AI.

We often describe this as helping customers optimize the "run" so they can invest more confidently in the "grow". In practice, that means reducing operational risk, increasing visibility, improving efficiency, and creating the right foundation for continuous innovation. For customers, the outcome is not just better uptime; it is a more agile SAP environment that supports long-term business change.

 

 

How do you experience the audits in practice, and what role do they play for your organization?

We see the audits as rigorous, constructive, and valuable. They are an important discipline for the organization because they require us to demonstrate - not just describe -  the maturity of our processes, controls, and service delivery practices.

In practice, audits help us maintain a strong level of operational consistency across teams and regions. They also create a useful feedback loop, highlighting where we are strong and where we can continue to improve. Internally, that reinforces accountability and continuous improvement; externally, it gives customers and SAP additional confidence in the quality and reliability of our services.

So for us, audits are not just compliance exercises. They are an important part of how we sustain operational excellence at scale.

 

For the first time, you achieved global certification for SAP security operations and SAP Business AI operations - can you tell us how your competencies around Security and Business AI operations are adding value to your service offerings?

These certifications are important because they reflect the evolving customer expectations. Today, customers want innovation, but they also want it delivered in a way that is secure, governed, and operationally robust. On the security side, our capabilities help customers strengthen the protection of their SAP landscapes through a combination of foundational controls, continuous monitoring, vulnerability management, privileged access governance, and broader security operations support. That adds value by reducing risk, improving visibility, and helping customers operate with greater confidence in increasingly complex hybrid environments.

On the Business AI side, the value is about helping customers move from interest to operational reality. It is one thing to explore AI use cases; it is another to run them reliably in enterprise environments. Our role is to help customers embed AI into their SAP landscape in a scalable, supportable way that is aligned with operational best practices. Together, Security and Business AI operations allow us to help customers innovate more safely and more effectively.

 

As SAP is strongly focusing on the cloud and encourages partners to support customers in their migration journey, how are you already helping customers in this area - and looking three to five years ahead, what role do SAP BTP and AI play in Syntax's vision and how do you expect your capabilities to evolve?

We are already supporting customers across the full cloud migration journey: from assessment and planning through migration, conversion, upgrade, and post-go-live operations. That includes helping customers modernize existing SAP environments, move to cloud-based operating models, and build the governance and support structures required for long-term success.

A major part of our value is that we do not view migration as a one-time project. We see it as part of a broader transformation of our operating model. Customers need a partner that can help them move, stabilize, optimize, and then continue to innovate.

Looking three to five years ahead, we see SAP BTP playing a central role as the platform for clean-core extensions, integration, automation, and data-driven innovation. We also expect AI to become much more deeply embedded - both in business processes and in the way SAP environments are operated and optimized. For Syntax, that means continuing to evolve our capabilities in platform operations, cloud governance, automation, observability, security, and AI-enabled service delivery. Our vision is to help customers build SAP operating models that are not only cloud-ready, but also innovation-ready.

 

If you could give one piece of advice to a customer who is just beginning to think about their SAP operations strategy for the next decade, what would it be?

If we had to highlight one key recommendation, it would be this: treat SAP operations as a strategic business capability rather than just a technical support function.

Over the next decade, organizations will need to balance resilience, security, cost efficiency, and continuous innovation far more closely than before. Those that succeed will be the ones who design their operating models early on, with standardization, automation, cloud readiness, and strong governance built in from the outset.

Ultimately, it is important not to separate your operations strategy from your transformation strategy. Reliable and well-governed operations are not just the foundation for stability - they are what make sustainable innovation possible.

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners, as well as an overview of all certified partners, can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: SSC v2+ ESM = How to Track API Usage Daily and Avoid 429 Rate Limiting Errors
entry.content:
Background

API rate limiting is one of the most common challenges developers face when integrating systems—especially in high-volume enterprise landscapes like SAP Integration Suite, Calling V2 APIs for Extensibility, Side by Side BTP extensions built or consuming in external SaaS platforms.

If not monitored properly, your application can start failing with HTTP 429 – Too Many Requests, impacting business-critical processes.

Previous Article : https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/rate-limiting-on-sap-sales-amp-service-cloud-v2-apis-must-to-know/ba-p/14024861 

Understanding 429 in SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 + ESM

SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 (CNS v2 stack) APIs enforce rate limits to protect system stability.

Typical causes of 429:

  • High-frequency polling (e.g., ticket sync in ESM)
  • Bulk data replication (master data, business partners, cases, products,  sales orders, sales quote, pricing, activities)
  • Parallel job execution from CPI or middleware
  • Extensibility Patterns (External Hooks, Autoflows...)
  • UI extensions triggering backend APIs excessively

To mointor your API usage before it turns into 429 - You, as an administrator, can monitor API calls made to your tenant to track on daily basis.

Where API Usage Happens in ESM & SAP SSC v2

Common integration points:

ScenarioAPI Usage Pattern
ESM Ticket IntegrationFrequent reads/writes on ticket APIs
S/4HANA replicationBurst loads (bulk API calls)
External service providersContinuous API traffic
CAP / BTP appsReal-time API triggering
CPI iFlowsBatch or scheduled calls
 

Where to Track API Usage

Find the API Metrics dashboard in system settings.
Go to your user menu and choose: System Settings >  Usage and Statistics > API Metrics.

The API Metrics dashboard is currently available by request only.
To activate this feature in your tenant, submit a support ticket requesting access using the component: CEC-CRM-CO-OPS.

  • The daily API limit for a test tenant is 30,000.
  • The daily API limit for a production tenant is 150,000 plus 1,500 times the total number of unit licenses.

For example: a tenant with 400 licenses has a limit of 150,000 plus 1,500 times 400, which equals 750,000.

Note
  • For SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2, a unit represents one (1) full licensed user.
  • For SAP Enterprise Service Management, a unit represents a block of 1000 transactions.

Official Documentation on API Metrics : Link

entry.title: Re: HTTP 403 Forbidden when SAP CPI calls On-Premise HTTP...
entry.content:
Hi,

When Cloud Connector triggers a 403 error the message is something like "Access denied to system <host>:<port>. In case this was a valid request, ensure to expose the system correctly in your cloud connector". In your case, I suggest you review the credentials and permissions of the user you are using to connect with SAP.

Hope it helps.

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Re: Background Job for ME05
entry.content: Thanks for Valuable reply, sir. Yes, It doesn't work.

entry.title: Re: MDK app button bound to key
entry.content: any news on this?

entry.title: Re: Background Job for ME05
entry.content: Ok, so my suggestion is: 1. Test if it works with the "delete" option. 2. Check whether the user mentioned in the Background Job Step has the necessary authorization.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Custom Code Migration Agent - Accelerating ECC to SAP S/4HANA Transformation with Agenti
entry.content:
Hi @fischerm ,

please try to click again the Apply buttion  under Windows->Preferences ->ABAP Development ->MCP Server. You should see the message

After that you can try again use MCP tools. 

Thanks and kind Regards,
Olga.
